title: DUX v9.5 UserOutcome Object
description: A User Outcome tracks what measurable benefit a user gets from engaging in specific behaviors within a user flow. Think of it as 'When users do X behaviors in this flow, they achieve Y outcome.' The key signals that indicate success are automatically inherited from the behaviors in the linked user flow - you don't have to guess what to measure, it's already defined by the flow's behaviors.

object_type.description: Object type discriminator - always 'UserOutcome' for UserOutcome objects

outcome_who.description: Who is the user that achieves this outcome? Example: 'Joel, the cluster admin'

outcome_behavior.description: What behavior does the user perform? This should align with the key behaviors in the linked user flow. Example: 'is able to efficiently track and control GPU access and usage across the cluster'

degree_of_signal_change.description: How much should the inherited signals change to indicate success? This is the measure of the outcome. Example: 'reducing the time it takes to allocate resources by 20%'

outcome_statement.description: The complete human-readable outcome statement constructed from: [outcome_who] + [outcome_behavior] + [degree_of_signal_change]. Example: 'Joel, the cluster admin, is able to efficiently track and control GPU access and usage across the cluster, reducing the time it takes to allocate resources by 20%.'

user_scenario.description: High-level scenario or context for this outcome

target_impact_when_achieved.description: Expected business or user impact when this outcome is achieved

priority.description: Business priority level for this outcome

reference_context.description: LLM-generated explanation of why this relationship exists

flow_ids.description: Which user flows contain the behaviors that lead to this outcome? When you link a user flow here, the key_signals field automatically gets populated with the signals from those behaviors. This is where the inheritance happens - you're saying 'the behaviors in this flow lead to this outcome, so measure those behaviors to track success.'

reference_context.description: LLM-generated explanation of why this relationship exists

problem_ids.description: Array of Problem IDs that this outcome addresses

reference_context.description: LLM-generated explanation of why this relationship exists

result_ids.description: Array of Result IDs that contribute to this outcome

end_user.description: User personas or roles who experience this outcome

key_signals.description: These signals are automatically inherited from the 'key behaviors' identified in the linked user flow. Each signal corresponds to a measurable behavior that indicates progress toward the outcome. The degree_of_signal_change field specifies how much these signals should change to indicate success. If no user flow is linked, this field is optional and the ETL pipeline can suggest signals based on the outcome_behavior.

acceptance_criteria.description: Clear, testable criteria that define successful achievement of this outcome

evidence_maturity.description: Progressive evidence maturity tier: 01_assumptive (inferred, no direct support), 02_anecdotal (1-2 qualitative signals), 03_early_signal (recurring signal, no quant), 04_balanced_signal (qualitative + quantitative), 05_triangulated (cross-method validation)

evidence.description: Array of Provenance IDs that support this outcome
